homepage_url_justification: Project homepage is the pkgdown site https://bart-turczynski.github.io/punycoder/ (also linked from DESCRIPTION URL).
sites_https_justification: All project sites are served over HTTPS: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder , https://bart-turczynski.github.io/punycoder/ , and https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=punycoder .
description_good_justification: DESCRIPTION and README provide a clear, succinct description of what punycoder does: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder#readme
interact_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md explains how to interact with and contribute to the project: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
contribution_requirements_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md states the requirements for acceptable contributions (tests, passing lint via the .lintr style config, and the verify CI gate): https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
license_location_justification: LICENSE is at the repository top level: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/blob/main/LICENSE
floss_license_osi_justification: MIT is an OSI-approved open source license: https://opensource.org/license/mit
documentation_basics_justification: Basic documentation in the README and the pkgdown site, plus package vignettes: https://bart-turczynski.github.io/punycoder/
documentation_interface_justification: Every exported function is documented (roxygen2 man pages) and published on the pkgdown reference index: https://bart-turczynski.github.io/punycoder/reference/index.html
repo_track_justification: All changes are tracked in git: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/commits/main
repo_interim_justification: Interim development commits are publicly available on GitHub between releases: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/commits/main
repo_distributed_justification: Uses git, a distributed version control system: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder
version_unique_justification: Each release carries a unique version (DESCRIPTION Version; CRAN enforces uniqueness): https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=punycoder
version_semver_justification: Uses semantic-style x.y.z versioning; releases visible at https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/releases
version_tags_justification: Releases are tagged in git and published as GitHub Releases: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/releases
release_notes_justification: NEWS.md documents user-facing changes for each release: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/blob/main/NEWS.md
release_notes_vulns_justification: No publicly known vulnerabilities have been fixed to date; NEWS.md records all changes: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/blob/main/NEWS.md
report_tracker_justification: GitHub Issues serve as the bug tracker: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/issues
report_process_justification: Users submit bug reports through the GitHub issue tracker: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/issues
report_responses_justification: Maintainer responds to issue reports on the tracker: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/issues
enhancement_responses_justification: Maintainer responds to enhancement requests on the tracker: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/issues
report_archive_justification: GitHub Issues provide a publicly available, searchable archive of reports and responses: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/issues?q=is%3Aissue
vulnerability_report_process_justificati: The vulnerability-reporting process is published in the security policy: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/security/policy (source: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/blob/main/SECURITY.md ). Reports go through GitHub private vulnerability reporting, with maintainer email as a documented fallback.
vulnerability_report_private_justificati: GitHub private vulnerability reporting (private security advisories) is enabled, providing a confidential channel: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/security/advisories/new
vulnerability_report_response_justificat: SECURITY.md commits to acknowledging reports within 7 days and coordinating a fix and disclosure: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/blob/main/SECURITY.md
build_justification: Builds with the standard R toolchain (R CMD build / INSTALL, devtools) plus the configure script that detects libidn2: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder
build_common_tools_justification: Uses the standard, widely available R build tooling plus a POSIX configure script.
build_floss_tools_justification: R, GCC/Clang, and the entire build toolchain are free/open source software: https://www.r-project.org/
test_invocation_justification: Tests run via devtools::test() and as part of R CMD check.
test_most_justification: Coverage is measured with covr and reported to Codecov: https://app.codecov.io/gh/bart-turczynski/punycoder
test_policy_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md requires tests for new features and bug fixes: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
tests_are_added_justification: New functionality ships with tests, enforced in review.
tests_documented_added_justification: The test-addition policy is documented in CONTRIBUTING.md: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
warnings_justification: Warnings are surfaced via R CMD check and lintr in CI: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/actions/workflows/verify.yml
warnings_fixed_justification: Warnings are treated as errors and fixed; R CMD check is kept clean.
warnings_strict_justification: CI sets LINTR_ERROR_ON_LINT=true so any lint fails the build: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/blob/main/.github/workflows/verify.yml
know_secure_design_justification: The maintainer applies secure design principles; punycoder defensively handles untrusted Punycode/IDNA input through its C/C++ codec core, enforcing length limits, valid UTF-8, and label-structure rules.
know_common_errors_justification: The maintainer is aware of common C/C++ memory-safety and IDNA parsing pitfalls (buffer overflows, integer overflows, homograph attacks). The C++ codec core is covered by clang-ASAN/UBSAN and valgrind in dynamic analysis.
crypto_password_storage_justification: punycoder stores no passwords or credentials.
crypto_random_justification: punycoder does not generate security-sensitive random values.
crypto_used_network_justification: punycoder is a string/IDNA library and does not itself make network connections.
crypto_tls12_justification: punycoder does not establish network connections.
crypto_certificate_verification_justific: punycoder does not establish network connections.
crypto_verification_private_justificatio: punycoder does not establish network connections.
delivery_mitm_justification: Distributed via CRAN and GitHub over HTTPS; CRAN publishes per-package checksums: https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=punycoder
delivery_unsigned_justification: All distribution downloads are served over HTTPS (CRAN, GitHub, r-universe): https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=punycoder
vulnerabilities_fixed_60_days_justificat: No outstanding known vulnerabilities; tracker at https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/issues
static_analysis_justification: lintr (full-package lint), R CMD check, and goodpractice are run; FOSSA and Socket scan dependencies: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/blob/main/.github/workflows/verify.yml
static_analysis_common_vulnerabilities_j: R CMD check plus lintr cover common defect classes; FOSSA/Socket cover dependency vulnerabilities; R CMD check --as-cran flags C/C++ compiler warnings: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/actions/workflows/verify.yml
static_analysis_fixed_justification: Static-analysis findings are addressed before merge.
static_analysis_often_justification: lintr runs on every CI build (each push and pull request): https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/blob/main/.github/workflows/verify.yml
dynamic_analysis_justification: Before releases the rhub workflow runs R CMD check — including the testthat suite — on the clang-ASAN/UBSAN and valgrind containers, exercising the C/C++ Punycode/IDNA codec core: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/blob/main/.github/workflows/rhub.yaml
dynamic_analysis_unsafe_justification: punycoder ships C/C++ code in src/ (the RFC 3492 Punycode encoder/decoder and UTS #46 normalizer). The rhub workflow runs the package under clang-ASAN/UBSAN (memory errors and undefined behaviour) and valgrind (memory leaks and illegal memory access) before releases: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/blob/main/.github/workflows/rhub.yaml
dynamic_analysis_enable_assertions_justi: punycoder's automated test suite uses testthat, whose expectations (expect_*) are assertions checked at runtime during dynamic analysis on every CI run: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/tree/main/tests/testthat . The rhub workflow additionally runs the package under the clang-ASAN/UBSAN and valgrind configurations, enabling runtime memory and undefined-behaviour assertions over the C/C++ codec core: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/blob/main/.github/workflows/rhub.yaml
dynamic_analysis_fixed_justification: Any issues surfaced by the sanitizer or valgrind runs are fixed before release.
test_continuous_integration_justificatio: GitHub Actions (verify.yml) runs lint + R CMD check + coverage on every push and pull request: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/actions/workflows/verify.yml
discussion_justification: GitHub Issues provide a public forum for project discussion: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/issues
no_leaked_credentials_justification: No credentials are committed to the repository; Socket and FOSSA scan the repo: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder
english_justification: Documentation and issue-tracker interaction are conducted in English (Language: en-US): https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder#readme
hardening_justification: Hardening mechanisms (e.g. HTTP security headers) apply to web applications; punycoder is an R library.
sites_password_security_justification: Project sites (GitHub, CRAN) manage their own authentication; punycoder operates no site with user passwords.
maintained_justification: Actively maintained: recent CRAN releases, frequent commits, and responsive issue handling: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/punycoder/commits/main
